multiplechoiceresponse.label: Which three types of post hoc explanation methods are commonly used to interpret complex AI models?

choice.div: Symbolic reasoning systems, reinforcement learning policies, and human memory recall.
Visualization of training losses, parameter tuning schedules, and data augmentation pipelines.
Global explanations (e.g., feature importance), local explanations (e.g., instance-level analysis), and counterfactuals (what-if scenarios).
Interactive dashboards, user surveys, and crowdsourced annotations.

div.p: The transcript explains that for black-box models such as deep neural networks, post hoc explanation techniques are essential. These include global explanations (how the model behaves overall, e.g., feature importance), local explanations (focusing on individual predictions), and counterfactuals (what-if scenarios showing how changes in inputs affect outcomes).
